properties.description: Leave the lights of Keswick & take a short easy stroll to see the stars in the sky & lake reflections. Perfect for families & astronomy novices, Friars Crag is one of the most scenic stargazing spots in the Lake District. Dont forget binoculars & a torch.
properties.name: Stargazing Walk at Friars Crag, North Lakes Outdoors

properties.description: A gentle walk with time & space to reflect. Peace How looks over Derwent Water to the north, & Castle Crag – a WW1 memorial fell – to the south. It was gifted to NT in 1917, so that anyone affected by the WW1 could come & experience peace & tranquility.
properties.from: High Brandelhow Jetty, Manesty, Borrowdale, CA12 5UG;NY252195
properties.name: Peace How Walk From High Brandelhow Jetty, North Lakes Outdoors
properties.to: High Brandelhow Jetty, Manesty, Borrowdale, CA12 5UG;NY252195

properties.description: Through valleys, over streams & up to spectacular viewpoints, the landscape here near Derwent Water, the ‘Jewel of the Lake District’ is magnificent in every season. Enjoy the timeless beauty of Ashness Bridge, the lake & Skiddaw as a backdrop.
properties.from: Walla Crag, Great Wood, Borrowdale, Lake District, Cumbria, CA12 5UP;NY271214
properties.name: Walla Crag to Ashness Bridge walk, North Lakes Outdoors
properties.to: Walla Crag, Great Wood, Borrowdale, Lake District, Cumbria, CA12 5UP;NY271214

properties.description: Linear there and back walk along Coledale Beck to Force Crag Mine.
properties.from: Aira Force Car Park, Whinlatter Pass, Above Derwent, Braithwaite, CA125RZ;NY227237
properties.name: Force Crag Mine Accessible Trail, North Lakes Outdoors
properties.to: Aira Force Car Park, Whinlatter Pass, Above Derwent, Braithwaite, CA125RZ;NY227237

properties.name: St John's in the Vale Centre
St John's in the Vale Centre
Lodore House
properties.note: Part of Lodore Falls Hotel next door

properties.name: Centenary Stone
properties.note: A large egg shaped split boulder of local Borrowdale volcanic lava put here to mark 100 years of the National Trust in the Lake District. the internal faces are carved mazes
